view_meta: how each target routes to a governed effect lane — resolutions, launches, deferred verdicts, and per-lane coverage (spec/017 · non-secret · read-only).
purpose: REGIME surface (record domain, spec/017 T-017-7, REQ-1716): the operator window onto the Actuation Regime Engine, rendered LIVE from the read-only GET /v1/regime read surface (RegimePage). Five honest sections grounded field-for-field in core/httpapi/regime.go: (1) mode & posture — a hero card DERIVED honestly from the worker mutation gate (liveState.mutation) + the documented Shadow-empties-the-audits contract, never an asserted served enum (the payload carries NO mode field); (2) lane coverage — the per-lane roll-up (lane, resolutions, actuations) with an activity bar and a counts-derived state (actuating/routing/idle), plus the fail-closed-refusal note that a resolved regime with no wired lane refuses; (3) per-target resolution — the append-only regime_resolution audit (target -> regime -> lane, rule_id, outcome resolved/refused); (4) launches — the regime_actuation audit (action_id, lane, job_template_id, op_class, job_id, token_ref rendered as a SecretRef REFERENCE never a value, created_at); (5) deferred verdicts — the deferred_verdict audit (action_id, job_id, terminal AWX status, verdict match/deviation/unverified, graduation verified_clean/deviated/no_credit).
notes: Honesty contract mirrors the skills surface: a clearly-labeled FIXTURE (RGM_FIX) renders the shape in preview; on a live session the four served arrays replace it; a 503 fail-closed endpoint renders an honest error state and NEVER the fixture (fabricating actuation-audit rows is exactly what INV-15 forbids); an empty spine (Shadow, before the flip) renders honest per-section empty states. NON-SECRET by construction (INV-13): target/regime/lane slugs, rule ids, op-classes, AWX job ids, verdict/graduation slugs, and the AWX token only as a SecretRef reference (token_ref, e.g. store:awx.token) — never a token value; the surface has no control that requests or displays key material. Read-only: no control selects a lane or launches a job. Reuses the console design system verbatim (rgm- prefix over tokens/chips/tbl/wrapcard/tiles/sec/empty; map-vs-territory plane language: map=indigo for the machine/effect lane, territory=steel for the observed target); tables live in their own overflow-x:auto container so the page body never scrolls sideways. Live state (liveState.regime — undefined pre-adopt, null on 503, RegimePage when served) is populated by modules/_live/js.txt liveAdopt().
notes_drift: RESOLVED (#55): the deployed console is deploy/console/v2/index.html (the Dockerfile copies ONLY index.html). It was formerly hand-maintained AHEAD of the assemble.py pipeline (the skills+wiki modules and the login-gate/posture-unknown live-layer fix lived only in index.html). Those surfaces are now back-ported into source (modules/skills/, modules/wiki/, modules/_live/js.txt, console.html), and assemble.py reproduces the deployed index.html BYTE-FOR-BYTE — verified by `make console-verify` and the CI console-drift job. index.html is now a build artifact of this pipeline: edit console.html/modules/ and re-run assemble.py; never hand-edit index.html.
verified: node --check on the extracted bundle passes; a jsdom render harness exercises views.regime() across preview (FIXTURE), live (served RegimePage, mutation-OFF READ-ONLY posture, idle-lane state), and 503 fail-closed (honest error, no fixture) states — 0 exceptions; hash-route #regime registers, VIEW_META.regime + the static nav entry resolve.
